text.title: Exploratory Charter Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that degrade exploratory testing sessions. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Mission drift — explorer starts on the email-onboarding flow and ends 70 minutes later debugging the billing API because they found a bug there.
failure-mode.detector: observation_log entries 30+ minutes into the session reference a target outside the charter's target field
failure-mode.repair: When the explorer feels the urge to chase a tangent, they log the question in `follow_up_questions` and stay on the charter. The tangent becomes a separate charter for a future session. Self-discipline; debrief flags drift as a finding.

failure-mode.description: Reconstructed log — explorer takes notes in their head, writes them up after the session, loses the load-bearing detail.
failure-mode.detector: observation_log entries are clustered at the end of the time_box OR contain no timestamps OR include the same input multiple times with different framings
failure-mode.repair: Use a structured logging tool (Notion, Logseq, plain Markdown with timestamp shortcut) during the session. Pair-exploration is the cheapest fix: one person explores, the other logs in real time.

failure-mode.description: Multi-tasking — explorer answers Slack, joins meetings, takes a customer support call during the session, breaking the cognitive thread.
failure-mode.detector: time_box passed but session debrief is < 30% of expected output OR explorer reports being interrupted
failure-mode.repair: Sessions are calendar-blocked with "do not disturb" status. Phone notifications off. If interruption is unavoidable, end the session early and reschedule with the remaining time as a fresh charter.

failure-mode.description: No debrief or late debrief — explorer files bugs but never writes the debrief, so themes and patterns are lost.
failure-mode.detector: session id present in the index but debrief.published_at empty OR more than 24 hours after session end
failure-mode.repair: Calendar reminder T+1 hour to write the debrief. If the explorer genuinely cannot debrief within 24h, the session is partially invalidated and the bug list survives but the patterns are lost. Schedule a quick retro to capture them while still fresh.

failure-mode.description: Scripted-mode creep — over time, explorers default to running the same set of test ideas across every session, the work becomes regression testing in disguise.
failure-mode.detector: median observation_log entries per session converges to the same set across sessions OR explorers use a personal checklist
failure-mode.repair: Promote the recurring test ideas to automated regression tests. Re-train explorers on heuristic-driven idea generation (SFDPOT, input variety) so new sessions generate fresh ideas.

failure-mode.description: Solo bias — engineer who wrote the feature explores it themselves, misses the same blind spots their tests have, finds nothing.
failure-mode.detector: explorer field matches the feature's primary author AND session yields zero bugs of severity >= medium
failure-mode.repair: Cross-team explorer assignment: explorer is not the author. Pair-exploration is acceptable. Author can debrief the explorer after the session but does not lead the exploration.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Feature author saying "you should focus on these areas" before the session may bias the explorer toward already-known weak spots. The charter sets focus areas based on risk and AC mapping, not author guidance. Author input is debrief material, not pre-session steering.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Monthly: testing lead reviews the trailing 4 weeks of charters and debriefs. Looks for: average time-to-debrief, theme-vs-buglist ratio, drift patterns, missions that consistently yield nothing (signal to retire or refocus). Adjusts the heuristic cheatsheet based on what is working.
